title: Fanout Exchanges and True Publish/Subscribe · Inference Brief: Durable Subscription Backlog
summary: Audit, notification, search, and analytics consumers share one queue and steal work from each other; a fanout exchange exists but late or disconnected subscriber behavior is undocumented. Planned artifact: Add and verify the durable subscription backlog increment in fanout subscription topology and lifecycle map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design durable subscription queues with retention, offline backlog, capacity, replay alternatives, and ownership limits.
Design exclusive server-named subscriptions for session-scoped state and account for disconnect, binding, declaration, and cleanup races.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Durable Subscription Backlog · Theory Durable Subscription Backlog
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 3790 in a benefit-notification consumer near its database pool limit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove one subscription queue per responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then exhaust the retry age before the attempt count reaches its maximum. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Durable Subscription Backlog contract for the supplied data.
